topic: Days, Months, Dates, and Grammar (AVOIR & QUEL)

subUnits.title: 3.1 Days of the Week, Months, and Seasons
notes: Days of the Week, Months, and Seasons
Key Concepts:
1. Days of the Week (Les Jours de la Semaine):
- lundi (Monday), mardi (Tuesday), mercredi (Wednesday), jeudi (Thursday), vendredi (Friday), samedi (Saturday), dimanche (Sunday).
- The French week starts on MONDAY (lundi).
- IMPORTANT: Days are NEVER capitalized in French unless they start a sentence!
- To say 'every Monday', use 'Le' + day: Le lundi, j'ai cours. (On Mondays, I have class).
2. Months of the Year (Les Mois de l'Année):
- janvier, février, mars, avril, mai, juin, juillet, août, septembre, octobre, novembre, décembre.
- IMPORTANT: Months are NEVER capitalized in French!
3. Seasons (Les Saisons):
- le printemps (spring), l'été (summer), l'automne (autumn/fall), l'hiver (winter).
- Use 'en' + season: en printemps, en été, en automne, en hiver.
Nigerian Context:
- Nigeria's Independence Day is on the 1st of October: le premier octobre.
- Knowing the days and months is essential for filling out forms, making appointments, and discussing schedules in French.
diagramDescriptions: Calendar layout with French days and months
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS1 French days months seasons pronunciation site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the first day of the week in French?
quiz.explanation: The French week starts on Monday ('lundi').

quiz.q: Are days of the week capitalized in French?
opts: No, unless they start a sentence
quiz.explanation: Days are lowercase in French unless they begin a sentence.

quiz.q: How do you say 'Today is Wednesday'?

subUnits.title: 3.2 Dates, Years, and Time Expressions
notes: Dates, Years, and Time Expressions
Key Concepts:
1. The Complete Date Formula:
- le + DATE NUMBER + MONTH.
- Example: 14th July = le quatorze juillet.
- EXCEPTION: For the 1st, use 'premier' (not 'un'): le premier janvier.
2. Years (Les Années):
- Say the full number. Unlike English, French always reads the year as a complete number.
- 2025 = deux mille vingt-cinq.
- To say 'in [year]', use 'en' + year: en 2025.
3. Asking and Stating Dates:
- What is today's date? = Quelle est la date aujourd'hui?
- Today is the... = Nous sommes le... / C'est le...
- When is your birthday? = Quand est ton anniversaire?
- My birthday is the... = Mon anniversaire est le...
4. Time Expressions:
- aujourd'hui (today), demain (tomorrow), hier (yesterday).
- ce matin (this morning), cet après-midi (this afternoon), ce soir (this evening).
- la semaine prochaine (next week), la semaine dernière (last week).
Nigerian Context:
- Nigerian Independence Day: le premier octobre.
- Democracy Day: le douze juin.
- Christmas: le vingt-cinq décembre.
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS1 French dates years time expressions site:youtube.com

quiz.q: How do you write '14th July' in French?
quiz.explanation: The formula is 'le' + number + month: 'le quatorze juillet'.

quiz.q: How do you say the year '2025'?
quiz.explanation: 2025 is read as a full number: 'deux mille vingt-cinq'.

quiz.q: How do you ask 'What is today's date?'
quiz.explanation: 'Quelle est la date aujourd'hui?' asks for the date.

quiz.q: How do you say 'My birthday is the...'?
quiz.explanation: 'Mon anniversaire est le...' is the standard way to state your birthday date.

subUnits.title: 3.3 Grammar: The Verb AVOIR and Question Words (QUEL/QUELLE)
notes: Grammar: AVOIR and QUEL/QUELLE
Key Concepts:
1. The Verb AVOIR (to have) - Present Tense:
- j'ai (I have), tu as (you have), il/elle/on a (he/she/one has).
- nous avons (we have), vous avez (you have), ils/elles ont (they have).
2. AVOIR in Expressions (Where English uses 'to be'):
- J'ai faim. (I am hungry. Lit: I have hunger.)
- J'ai soif. (I am thirsty.)
- J'ai chaud. (I am hot.) / J'ai froid. (I am cold.)
- J'ai sommeil. (I am sleepy.) / J'ai peur. (I am afraid.)
3. Negation with AVOIR:
- Wrap with ne...pas. 'ne' becomes 'n'' before a vowel.
- Je n'ai pas le temps. (I don't have time.)
4. QUEL / QUELLE (What / Which):
- Used to ask about time, dates, ages. It must agree in gender and number with the noun!
- Quel (masc. sing.): Quel âge as-tu? (What age are you?)
- Quelle (fem. sing.): Quelle heure est-il? (What time is it?)
- NOTE: All four forms (quel, quelle, quels, quelles) are pronounced exactly the same: /kel/!
Nigerian Context:
- Using 'AVOIR' correctly is a major step in sounding like a native French speaker. Remember, in French, you 'have' hunger, you don't 'are' hungry!
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS1 French verb AVOIR conjugation expressions site:youtube.com

quiz.q: How do you say 'I am hungry' in French?
quiz.explanation: 'I am hungry' is 'J'ai faim.' (Literally: I have hunger).

quiz.q: How do you say 'I am cold'?
quiz.explanation: 'I am cold' is 'J'ai froid.' (Literally: I have cold).

quiz.q: How do you say 'I don't have time'?
opts: Je ne ai pas le temps.
quiz.explanation: 'I don't have time' is 'Je n'ai pas le temps.' ('ne' becomes 'n'' before 'ai').

quiz.q: How do you ask 'What time is it?' (feminine noun 'heure')?
quiz.explanation: 'heure' is feminine, so we use 'Quelle': 'Quelle heure est-il?'

quiz.q: How do you ask 'How old are you?' (masculine noun 'âge')?
quiz.explanation: 'âge' is masculine, so we use 'Quel': 'Quel âge as-tu?'

quiz.q: Do the four forms of QUEL (quel, quelle, quels, quelles) sound different when spoken?
opts: No, they all sound exactly the same (/kel/)
Only masculine and feminine sound different
Only singular and plural sound different
quiz.explanation: All four forms are pronounced exactly the same: /kel/. The difference is only in spelling.
